event: Russia's Klin loitering munition uses Western/Chinese components — validates supply chain porosity despite sanctions
evidence_basis: GUR identified 'wide range of foreign-made components' in Russian Klin UAV (May 4 article). Validates existing CRITICAL flag (RPi in Geran drones, 40K+ units diverted). Pattern: Russian procurement continues accessing dual-use Western components via transshipment.
probability_basis: Primary source (Ukrainian military intelligence teardown report). Confirms established diversion pattern documented in 6 existing CRITICAL flags for RPi/component diversion.
drivers: GUR Klin teardown confirms Western component integration
validates_flag_id: RPi 4B confirmed in Geran-2/3 'Borscht' tracker — 40,000+ units diverted

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Fourth confirmed Russian platform using Western dual-use electronics — indicates systemic procurement network

event: FPV component supply remains stable despite dual Ukraine/Drone Dominance demand — China export controls not yet enforced
evidence_basis: Parts DB shows FPV cameras: 187/338 Chinese origin, median $25. ESCs: 119/165 Chinese, median $45. Flight controllers: 267/328 use STM32. Trend deltas: all categories +0.0% price change. Validates existing WARNING flag understating China supply stability.
probability_basis: Live database count + zero price delta across 9 component categories. Strong inference: if dual-use export controls were tightening, we'd see price movement or availability gaps.
drivers: Zero price movement across FPV categories (ESCs, cameras, FCs)
Ukraine production 10K+/mo + Drone Dominance 200K units both use shared supply
validates_flag_id: Ukraine (26 platforms) and US Drone Dominance compete for same FPV components

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Stable pricing despite 200K+ unit Drone Dominance demand suggests China-origin FPV supply chain has excess capacity

event: DRAM price trajectory (+8.5% QoQ) will cascade to companion computer shortage Q3-Q4 2026 — RPi 5 16GB lead time extends to 16+ weeks
evidence_basis: Existing WARNING flag shows DDR4/LPDDR4X +8.5% QoQ, RPi 5 16GB jumped $120→$145. Companion computers DB: 10/25 US-origin, median $248. Jetson Orin NX ($599→$649) uses same LPDDR5. AI datacenter demand (driver per flag) continues H2 2026.
probability_basis: Strong inference from established QoQ price trend + known AI datacenter capacity allocation. Lead time estimate based on RPi Foundation historical constraint patterns (not primary source).
drivers: AI datacenter DRAM consumption continues (OpenAI/Anthropic H100 clusters)
RPi 5 and Jetson Orin NX both use constrained LPDDR capacity

event: Geran-4 jet drone intercept (May 3) indicates Russian production scaled — validates upstream component demand for jet propulsion FCs
evidence_basis: Ukraine shot down first Geran-4 jet drone May 3 (Wild Hornets). Existing CRITICAL flag notes Geran-5 uses RPi + 12-channel Kometa nav. Geran-4 operational debut implies production line active — drives demand for jet-rated flight controllers (likely STM32H7 series given Geran-5 precedent).
probability_basis: Credible signal: first combat intercept confirms production, but we lack teardown data on Geran-4 internals. Inference: if Geran-5 uses RPi + STM32, Geran-4 likely uses similar architecture.
drivers: First Geran-4 intercept May 3 confirms operational deployment
Geran-5 precedent (RPi + Kometa nav + mesh radio) suggests shared component base

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Third Geran variant (2/3/4/5) using similar electronics architecture — indicates standardized procurement

event: STM32H743 supply tightens Q3 2026 — Ukraine FPV production (10K+/mo) + Drone Dominance (200K) + Blue UAS (Skydio/Teal/ModalAI) compete for same MCU
evidence_basis: Existing WARNING flag: STM32H743 in 13 platforms across 5 programs (Drone Dominance, USMC FPV, Army SRR, Ukraine). Flight controllers DB: 267/328 use STM32 (81%). Parts DB shows 328 FCs tracked, no Chinese alternative at scale except AT32 (not NDAA-compliant). Compound demand from three simultaneous large-scale programs.
probability_basis: Strong inference from live DB count + three confirmed high-volume programs using same MCU family. ST lead times historically extend under dual military/commercial demand. Medium confidence on Q3 timing (not primary source).
drivers: Ukraine 10K+/mo FPV production (22 Ukrainian FCs in DB)
Blue UAS programs (Skydio X10/X10D, Teal, ModalAI all use STM32H7)
validates_flag_id: STM32 concentration: 81% of 328 tracked FCs use ST MCU family (live DB count)

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Ukraine + Drone Dominance both standardized on STM32 — creates single point of failure for Western FPV capacity

event: Battery supply remains stable despite 720% Blue UAS cost premium — lithium pricing detached from compliance procurement
evidence_basis: Parts DB: 219 batteries tracked, 102/219 US-origin, median $30. Trend delta: batteries +0.0% price change. Existing WARNING flag: Blue UAS BOM $4,348 vs non-compliant $530 (720% premium), but battery cost is NOT a major driver (frame $400, mesh $1,800, companion $599 are top costs). Battery pricing stable.
probability_basis: Primary source (live parts DB + zero price delta). Validates that Blue UAS cost premium is driven by specialized components (mesh radios, NDAA chips), not commodity batteries.
drivers: Zero price movement in battery category (median $30 stable)
102/219 batteries US-origin (47%) — adequate domestic supply for compliance
validates_flag_id: Blue UAS BOM cost index: $4,348 — 720% premium over non-compliant build
